record.field: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) is the apex financial institution in Nigeria, responsible for the regulation and supervision of the banking sector.
The Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation (NDIC) provides deposit insurance coverage for bank depositors and promotes public confidence in the Nigerian banking system.
The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is the regulator of the Nigerian capital market, ensuring that the market is fair, transparent, and efficient.
The National Insurance Commission (NAICOM) regulates the insurance sector in Nigeria, ensuring the protection of policyholders and the stability of the insurance market.
The Financial Reporting Council of Nigeria (FRCN) sets accounting and financial reporting standards in Nigeria, ensuring the accuracy and reliability of financial information.
The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) is responsible for investigating and prosecuting financial crimes, including fraud, money laundering, and corruption in Nigeria.
The Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) regulates the telecommunications industry in Nigeria, ensuring fair competition and protecting consumer rights.
The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) is responsible for the regulation and management of companies in Nigeria, including registration and oversight of corporate entities.
The Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) is responsible for the assessment, collection, and accounting of federal taxes in Nigeria.

The Nigerian Financial Intelligence Unit (NFIU) is the central agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating financial intelligence to combat money laundering and terrorist financing.
